annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: (Release Point 117-285not263)

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: TITLE 1-GENERAL PROVISIONS

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: This title was enacted by act July 30, 1947, ch. 388, §1, 61 Stat. 633

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: STATUTORY NOTES AND RELATED SUBSIDIARIES

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: POSITIVE LAW; CITATION

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: This title has been made positive law by section 1 of act July 30, 1947, ch. 388, 61 Stat. 633, which provided in part that: "Title 1 of the United States Code entitled 'General Provisions', is codified and enacted into positive law and may be cited as '1 U. S. C., §--.' "

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: REPEALS

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: Act July 30, 1947, ch. 388, §2, 61 Stat. 640, provided that the sections or parts thereof of the Statutes at Large or the Revised Statutes covering provisions codified in this Act are repealed insofar as the provisions appeared in former Title 1, and provided that any rights or liabilities now existing under the repealed sections or parts thereof shall not be affected by the repeal.

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: WRITS OF ERROR

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: Act June 25, 1948, ch. 646, §23, 62 Stat. 990, provided that: "All Acts of Congress referring to writs of error shall be construed as amended to the extent necessary to substitute appeal for writ of error."

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: TABLE SHOWING DISPOSITION OF ALL SECTIONS OF FORMER TITLE 1

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: CHAPTER 1-RULES OF CONSTRUCTION

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: Sec.

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: Words denoting number, gender, etc. 1 1.

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: "County" as including "parish", etc. 1 2.

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: "Vessel" as including all means of water transportation. 3.

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: "Vehicle" as including all means of land transportation. 4.

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: "Company" or "association" as including successors and assigns. 5.

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: Limitation of term "products of American fisheries." 6.

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: Definition of "marriage" and "spouse". 2 7.

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: "Person", "human being", "child", and "individual" as including born-alive infant. 8.

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: EDITORIAL NOTES

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: AMENDMENTS

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: So in original. Does not conform to section catchline. 1

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: 2  Section catchline amended by Pub. L. 117-228 without corresponding amendment of chapter analysis.

annotations.label: page_footer
annotations.rawText: §1. Words denoting number, gender, and so forth

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: In determining the meaning of any Act of Congress, unless the context indicates otherwisewords importing the singular include and apply to several persons, parties, or things; words importing the plural include the singular;

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: words importing the masculine gender include the feminine as well;

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: words used in the present tense include the future as well as the present;

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: the words "insane" and "insane person" shall include every idiot, insane person, and person non compos mentis;

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: the words "person" and "whoever" include corporations, companies, associations, firms, partnerships, societies, and joint stock companies, as well as individuals;

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: "officer" includes any person authorized by law to perform the duties of the office;

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: "signature" or "subscription" includes a mark when the person making the same intended it as such;

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: "oath" includes affirmation, and "sworn" includes affirmed;

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: "writing" includes printing and typewriting and reproductions of visual symbols by photographing, multigraphing, mimeographing, manifolding, or otherwise.

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: EDITORIAL NOTES

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: AMENDMENTS

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: 2012 -Pub. L. 112-231, in fifth clause after opening clause, struck out "and 'lunatic' " before "shall include every" and "lunatic," before "insane person,".

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: 1951 -Act Oct. 31, 1951, substituted, in fourth clause after opening clause, "used" for "use".

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: 1948 -Act June 25, 1948, included "tense", "whoever", "signature", "subscription", "writing" and a broader definition of "person".

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: CONTINENTAL UNITED STATES

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: Pub. L. 86-70, §48, June 25, 1959, 73 Stat. 154, provided that: "Whenever the phrase 'continental United States' is used in any law of the United States enacted after the date of enactment of this Act [June 25, 1959], it shall mean the 49 States on the North American Continent and the District of Columbia, unless otherwise expressly provided."

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: §2. "County" as including "parish", and so forth

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: The word "county" includes a parish, or any other equivalent subdivision of a State or Territory of the United States.

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: §3. "Vessel" as including all means of water transportation

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: The word "vessel" includes every description of watercraft or other artificial contrivance used, or capable of being used, as a means of transportation on water.

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: §4. "Vehicle" as including all means of land transportation

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: The word "vehicle" includes every description of carriage or other artificial contrivance used, or capable of being used, as a means of transportation on land.

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: §5. "Company" or "association" as including successors and assigns

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: The word "company" or "association", when used in reference to a corporation, shall be deemed to embrace the words "successors and assigns of such company or association", in like manner as if these last-named words, or words of similar import, were expressed.

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: §6. Limitation of term "products of American fisheries"

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: Wherever, in the statutes of the United States or in the rulings, regulations, or interpretations of various administrative bureaus and agencies of the United States there appears or may appear the term "products of American fisheries" said term shall not include fresh or frozen fish fillets, fresh or frozen fish steaks, or fresh or frozen slices of fish substantially free of bone (including any of the foregoing divided into sections), produced in a foreign country or its territorial waters, in whole or in part with the use of the labor of persons who are not residents of the United States.

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: §7. Marriage

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: (a) For the purposes of any Federal law, rule, or regulation in which marital status is a factor, an individual shall be considered married if that individual's marriage is between 2 individuals and is valid in the State where the marriage was entered into or, in the case of a marriage entered into outside any State, if the marriage is between 2 individuals and is valid in the place where entered into and the marriage could have been entered into in a State.

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: (b) In this section, the term "State" means a State, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, or any other territory or possession of the United States.

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: (c) For purposes of subsection (a), in determining whether a marriage is valid in a State or the place where entered into, if outside of any State, only the law of the jurisdiction applicable at the time the marriage was entered into may be considered.

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: EDITORIAL NOTES

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: AMENDMENTS

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: 2022 -Pub. L. 117-228 amended section generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: "In determining the meaning of any Act of Congress, or of any ruling, regulation, or interpretation of the various administrative bureaus and agencies of the United States, the word 'marriage' means only a legal union between one man and one woman as husband and wife, and the word 'spouse' refers only to a person of the opposite sex who is a husband or a wife."

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: STATUTORY NOTES AND RELATED SUBSIDIARIES

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: SEVERABILITY

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: Pub. L. 117-228, §8, Dec. 13, 2022, 136 Stat. 2307, provided that: "If any provision of this Act [see Short Title of 2022 Amendment note set out under section 1 of this title], or any amendment made by this Act, or the application of such provision to any person, entity, government, or circumstance, is held to be unconstitutional, the remainder of this Act, or any amendment made thereby, or the application of such provision to all other persons, entities, governments, or circumstances, shall not be affected thereby."

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: FINDINGS

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: Pub. L. 117-228, §2, Dec. 13, 2022, 136 Stat. 2305, provided that: "Congress finds the following:

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: "(1) No union is more profound than marriage, for it embodies the highest ideals of love, fidelity, devotion, sacrifice, and family.

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: "(2) Diverse beliefs about the role of gender in marriage are held by reasonable and sincere people

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: based on decent and honorable religious or philosophical premises. Therefore, Congress affirms that such people and their diverse beliefs are due proper respect.

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: "(3) Millions of people, including interracial and same-sex couples, have entered into marriages and have enjoyed the rights and privileges associated with marriage. Couples joining in marriage deserve to have the dignity, stability, and ongoing protection that marriage affords to families and children."

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: NO IMPACT ON RELIGIOUS LIBERTY AND CONSCIENCE

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: Pub. L. 117-228, §6, Dec. 13, 2022, 136 Stat. 2306, provided that:

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: "(a) IN GENERAL.-Nothing in this Act [see Short Title of 2022 Amendment note set out under section 1 of this title], or any amendment made by this Act, shall be construed to diminish or abrogate a religious liberty or conscience protection otherwise available to an individual or organization under the Constitution of the United States or Federal law.

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: "(b) GOODS OR SERVICES.-Consistent with the First Amendment to the Constitution, nonprofit religious organizations, including churches, mosques, synagogues, temples, nondenominational ministries, interdenominational and ecumenical organizations, mission organizations, faith-based social agencies, religious educational institutions, and nonprofit entities whose principal purpose is the study, practice, or advancement of religion, and any employee of such an organization, shall not be required to provide services, accommodations, advantages, facilities, goods, or privileges for the solemnization or celebration of a marriage. Any refusal under this subsection to provide such services, accommodations, advantages, facilities, goods, or privileges shall not create any civil claim or cause of action."

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: STATUTORY PROHIBITION

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: Pub. L. 117-228, §7, Dec. 13, 2022, 136 Stat. 2306, provided that:

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: "(a) NO IMPACT ON STATUS AND BENEFITS NOT ARISING FROM A MARRIAGE.-Nothing in this Act [see Short Title of 2022 Amendment note set out under section 1 of this title], or any amendment made by this Act, shall be construed to deny or alter any benefit, status, or right of an otherwise eligible entity or person which does not arise from a marriage, including tax-exempt status, tax treatment, educational funding, or a grant, contract, agreement, guarantee, loan, scholarship, license, certification, accreditation, claim, or defense.

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: "(b) NO FEDERAL RECOGNITION OF POLYGAMOUS MARRIAGES.-Nothing in this Act, or any amendment made by this Act, shall be construed to require or authorize Federal recognition of marriages between more than 2 individuals."

annotations.label: section_header
annotations.rawText: §8. "Person", "human being", "child", and "individual" as including born-alive infant

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: (a) In determining the meaning of any Act of Congress, or of any ruling, regulation, or interpretation of the various administrative bureaus and agencies of the United States, the words "person", "human being", "child", and "individual", shall include every infant member of the species homo sapiens who is born alive at any stage of development.

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: (b) As used in this section, the term "born alive", with respect to a member of the species homo sapiens, means the complete expulsion or extraction from his or her mother of that member, at any stage of development, who after such expulsion or extraction breathes or has a beating heart, pulsation of the umbilical cord, or definite movement of voluntary muscles, regardless of whether the umbilical cord has been cut, and regardless of whether the expulsion or extraction occurs as a result of natural or induced labor, cesarean section, or induced abortion.

annotations.label: list_item
annotations.rawText: (c) Nothing in this section shall be construed to affirm, deny, expand, or contract any legal status or legal right applicable to any member of the species homo sapiens at any point prior to being "born alive" as defined in this section.

annotations.label: text
annotations.rawText: CHAPTER 2-ACTS AND RESOLUTIONS; FORMALITIES OF ENACTMENT; REPEALS; SEALING OF INSTRUMENTS
